Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Methods explicitly state the 32-bp deletion was in OPRIII-B1 (TraesCS1B02G018700).

Source_Species.reason: OPRIII-B1 is a wheat gene on 1BS; paper discusses wheat OPRIII-B1 and its 1BS introgression.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Paper identifies wheat as Triticum aestivum.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional tests were performed in wheat lines.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Experimental system is wheat, identified in paper as Triticum aestivum.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: OPRIII-B1 was tested in Hahn-1RW mutant background and UBI::OPRIII-B1 was expressed in Hahn-1RS and Fielder lines.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results state increased OPRIII dosage/expression is associated with increased JA and JA-Ile, and the study frames OPRIII as regulating a critical step in JA-Ile synthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: JA-Ile is described as an active phytohormone; it is an oxylipin-derived jasmonate.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper links altered JA-Ile to root architecture and drought/water-stress adaptation, though this is shown mostly at genotype/pathway level rather than B1 alone.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: OPRIII genes are explicitly described as enzymes involved in biosynthesis of JA and JA-Ile.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: In vitro assays show OPRIII proteins reduce OPDA, 13-epi-12-OPDA, and 4,5-ddh-JA, supporting placement at this reduction step.

Enzyme_Name.reason: OPRIII stands for 12-OXOPHYTODIENOATE REDUCTASE SUBFAMILY III.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is provided in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Paper explicitly places these genes in OPR subfamily III; OPRs are the old yellow enzyme family, though the exact phrase 'old yellow enzyme family' is not used.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Results show recombinant OPRIII proteins use OPDA, 13-epi-12-OPDA and 4,5-ddh-JA as substrates.

Primary_Product.reason: Paper shows substrate consumption and links OPRIII dosage to increased JA/JA-Ile, but does not directly name the immediate reduced products for OPRIII-B1.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: NADPH consumption assays with the listed substrates support NADPH-dependent reduction in the JA route.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: This exact classification term is not used, though the paper supports an upstream role leading to JA/JA-Ile accumulation.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Paper does not explicitly call the step 'midstream', though it is clearly an intermediate step in JA biosynthesis.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Discussion states OPRIII gene dosage is a sensitive regulatory point of the JA pathway, with single-gene knockouts altering phenotype.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Supplementary localization experiment is summarized in Results as nucleus and cytoplasm outside the peroxisome.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results state most OPRIII genes are higher at 6 than 16 DAG; OPRIII-B1 detected in 1RW but not 1RS roots; transgene expressed in leaves.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Transgenes were driven by the maize UBIQUITIN promoter.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: mut-OPRIII-B1 increased seminal root length; UBI::OPRIII-B1 reduced seminal root length; increased OPRIII dosage/expression is associated with higher JA/JA-Ile.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is supported, though hormone measurements are shown for 1RW and UBI::OPRIII-R5 rather than B1-specific lines.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed approaches appear in the paper for OPRIII-B1 directly or for OPRIII proteins/family context, with B1 specifically included in CRISPR, overexpression, enzyme and localization analyses.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Paper reports constitutive overexpression reduced leaf length and aerial dry weight; it also notes single mutants remained shorter than 1RS.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion explicitly proposes CRISPR/dosage fine-tuning of OPRIII genes to improve root growth in 1RS/1BS backgrounds while retaining quality.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Study includes hydroponics, water-limited field context, ibuprofen inhibitor and MeJA treatments.

Omics_Data.reason: All listed BioProject accessions are given in Data availability.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This caveat matches the paper: direct JA/JA-Ile quantification was for 1RW and UBI::OPRIII-R5, not mut-OPRIII-B1 or UBI::OPRIII-B1 specifically.

corrections.reason: No EC number is provided in the paper.

Source_Species.reason: Paper designates OPRIII-R1 as a rye-derived gene on 1RS.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Rye source species in paper is Secale cereale.

Applied_Species.reason: Mutant analyses were performed in wheat line Hahn-1RW carrying rye chromatin.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Applied species is wheat, Triticum aestivum.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: mut-OPRIII-R1 alleles were tested in Hahn-1RW.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The study links OPRIII genes, including rye copies, to JA/JA-Ile biosynthesis and increased JA/JA-Ile in high-dosage genotypes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: JA-Ile is described as an active phytohormone derived from jasmonates.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper links JA-Ile and OPRIII dosage to root architecture and water-stress adaptation at the genotype/pathway level.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: OPRIII genes are explicitly described as involved in JA/JA-Ile biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The branch is reasonable by family function, but OPRIII-R1 itself was not biochemically tested with these substrates in this paper.

Enzyme_Name.reason: R1 is designated an OPRIII gene, i.e., 12-oxophytodienoate reductase subfamily III.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is stated in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: R1 is classified in OPR subfamily III; the exact domain wording is not given but family placement is.

Primary_Substrate.reason: These substrates were shown for other OPRIII proteins, not specifically OPRIII-R1.

Primary_Product.reason: Immediate product of R1 was not directly measured or named.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This is inferred from family membership, not directly demonstrated for R1 in this paper.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Exact classification term not used; upstream contribution to JA/JA-Ile is inferred.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Paper supports OPRIII dosage as a sensitive regulatory point; R1 knockout of one duplicated copy altered root phenotype.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results say duplicated rye OPRIII genes showed higher expression in 1RW than 1RS, and most OPRIII genes were higher at 6 than 16 DAG.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: mut-OPRIII-R1.7 and mut-OPRIII-R1.3 increased seminal root length in 1RW.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The described deletions, truncations, and 17–23% longer roots are all reported.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: CRISPR-Cas9, NGS genotyping, and hydroponic phenotyping were used.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Paper notes single gene mutants were still shorter than 1RS, implying multiple copies contribute.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion supports editing OPRIII genes in 1RS/1BS recombinants to improve root growth under water stress.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: R1 mutant phenotyping was done in hydroponics.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Paper does not assay R1 enzyme directly; functional assignment is indirect from family and genetics.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: UBI::OPRIII-R5 was tested in Hahn-1RS and Fielder.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Root tips of UBI::OPRIII-R5 had increased JA and JA-Ile.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: JA-Ile is an active phytohormone in the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper links JA-Ile increases to root architecture and water-limited adaptation.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: OPRIII genes are implicated in JA/JA-Ile biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: R5 itself was not biochemically tested in vitro in this paper; the step is inferred from OPRIII family function.

Enzyme_Name.reason: R5 is designated an OPRIII gene.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is given in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: R5 belongs to OPR subfamily III according to the phylogenetic designation.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Substrates were demonstrated for other OPRIII proteins, not specifically R5.

Primary_Product.reason: Overexpression of R5 was associated with increased JA and JA-Ile, though immediate enzymatic products were not measured.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This reaction is inferred from subfamily function and not directly assayed for R5.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Exact term not used, though upstream contribution is supported.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Study supports OPRIII dosage/expression as a sensitive regulatory point in the JA pathway, including R5 overexpression effects.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Duplicated rye OPRIII genes are higher in 1RW than 1RS; R5 transgene expression in leaves was confirmed under the maize UBI promoter.

Applied_Promoters.reason: R5 overexpression lines used the maize UBIQUITIN promoter.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: UBI::OPRIII-R5 shortened seminal roots, brought secondary roots closer to the RAM, altered ROS pattern, and increased JA/JA-Ile.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four listed findings are described in Results and associated figures.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transgenic overexpression, hydroponics, hormone profiling, ROS staining, and transcriptomics were all performed for R5 lines.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Paper reports reduced leaf length and aerial dry weight in constitutive overexpression lines.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion concludes OPRIII genes are useful targets to engineer cereal root architecture, with caution implied by ectopic-expression penalties.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Hydroponics and JA-pathway pharmacology are part of the study context for linking OPRIII effects to JA.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq BioProjects for 1RS, 1RW and Hahn-UBI::OPRIII-R5 are listed in Data availability.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Paper does not directly assay recombinant R5 protein; link is supported by overexpression phenotype, metabolite data, and transcriptome similarity.
